content.problemStatement:
Chef has been working hard to compete in MasterChef.

He is ranked XXX out of all contestants. However, only 101010 contestants would be selected for the finals.

Check whether Chef made it to the top 101010 or not?


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of one integers XXX — the current rank of Chef.


Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line, `YES`, if Chef made it to the top 101010 and `NO` otherwise.


Each character of the output may be printed in either uppercase or lowercase. That is, the strings `NO`, `no`, `nO`, and `No` will be treated as equivalent.
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Each of the next T lines contains a single integer X, Chef's rank.
content.outputFormat: For each test case output on a new line "YES" if X ≤ 10, otherwise output "NO".

sampleTestCases.explanation: Ranks 15, 10, 1, and 50 are compared with 10. Only 10 and 1 are ≤ 10, so the answers are NO, YES, YES, NO respectively.
10 and 9 are within the top 10 (YES), 11 is not (NO).
Rank equals the cutoff, so Chef qualifies.

solution.approach: Read the number of test cases. For each test case read X and compare it with 10. If X is less than or equal to 10 print YES, else print NO. This is O(1) per test case.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T) – constant work for each of the T test cases.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) – only a few integer variables are used.
